buggy_code:
import pandas as pd

prices = pd.read_csv('prices.csv', index_col='date', parse_dates=True)

# Compute rolling means using only past data
sma_short = prices['close'].rolling(window=20).mean()
sma_long = prices['close'].rolling(window=50).mean()

# Signal uses lagged values — no lookahead
signal = (sma_short.shift(1) > sma_long.shift(1)).astype(int)

# Strategy returns
returns = prices['close'].pct_change()
strategy = signal * returns
clean_code:
import pandas as pd

prices = pd.read_csv('prices.csv', index_col='date', parse_dates=True)

sma_short = prices['close'].rolling(window=20).mean()
sma_long = prices['close'].rolling(window=50).mean()

signal = (sma_short.shift(1) > sma_long.shift(1)).astype(int)

returns = prices['close'].pct_change()
strategy = signal * returns
description: Clean example — correct rolling mean crossover with proper lagging.
explanation: Both SMAs are shifted by 1 before comparison, ensuring the signal at time T only uses information available up to T-1. No lookahead bias.
